executive_summary: Latest updates from 2 sources covering the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Monitoring 2 key developments. AI analysis refreshing shortly.
execSummaryRich: Escalation: HIGH. Key developments: Netanyahu admits to arming crime families and militias in Gaza to fight Hamas. Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drone strikes. Situation remains active.

key_developments.headline: Netanyahu admits to arming crime families and militias in Gaza to fight Hamas
key_developments.detail: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu admitted to arming crime families and militias in Gaza to fight Hamas in a statement following Yisrael Beytenu chair MK Avigdor Liberman's claims in an interview on KAN Reshet Bet Thursday. key_developments.headline: Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drone strikes
key_developments.detail: Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drone strikes Thick, black smoke rose from Kuwait International Airport Saturday after suspected Iranian drone strikes damaged radar systems and fuel storage facilities, state media said. No fatalities were reported. The airport has been repeatedly targeted since the US

terminology_explained.simple_explanation: Iran's elite Revolutionary Guard — controls missiles and drones.
Narrow waterway — 20% of world oil passes through it.

what_to_watch_next: Monitor live feeds for breaking developments.

india_impact.headline: India Monitors Gulf Conflict
india_impact.detail: India's energy security tied to Gulf stability.
india_impact.full_detail: India imports 85% of crude oil from Gulf. Strait of Hormuz disruption would immediately impact supply.

indiaSummary: India continues to monitor the US-Israel-Iran conflict closely given its significant dependence on Gulf energy imports and the large Indian diaspora. Oil price volatility directly impacts petrol prices for Indian consumers.
